data.title: Stock index futures tumble as Trump-backed bill fails to pass, fueling shutdown risks
data.description:
Stock index futures tumbled on Friday, after a stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump was rejected in the House of Representatives, further fueling concerns of a government shutdown.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.7% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.55%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.29%.

"The big question now is whether a U.S. government shutdown is about to happen," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The latest bill was voted down by 235 votes to 174. The funding is set to expire on Friday night.

"A short-lived shutdown affects government workers, but has limited economic impact. The longer a shutdown lasts, the more disruptive it is to the U.S. economy. Economic data may not get published in a shutdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Wall Street's bounce back on Thursday following its worst session in over three months ran out of steam in the final hour of trading.

"For those wanting a quiet run up to Xmas, the good news is that there hasn't been any real follow-through to the Fed-induced slump on Wednesday. The bad news is that an initial recovery in markets struggled to gain traction yesterday," Reid added.

Traders will also keep an eye on the November PCE price index data. Core PCE is expected to come in at 0.2% M/M and 2.9% Y/Y.

PCE is forecasted to come in at 2.5% Y/Y and 0.2% M/M.

"U.S. personal income and consumption data should continue to signal rising real incomes. The fact that household incomes are so strong, allowing consumption without use of savings or credit card debt, gives a solid foundation for US economic growth in 2025," Donovan added.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Sanofi to buy China rights to Cytokinetics lead asset
data.description:
• Cytokinetics (NASDAQ:CYTK) announced Friday that Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) would buy exclusive rights to its lead candidate, aficamten, in Greater China from its partner, Corxel Pharmaceuticals.
  • The company didn’t disclose the financial terms of the agreement between but said it remains on track to receive up to $150M in milestone payments and low-to-high teens on aficamten sales from the French drugmaker.
  • Cytokinetics (NASDAQ:CYTK) added that it is entitled to receive additional undisclosed payments linked to the agreement between the two.
  • In 2020, Shanghai-based Corxel teamed up with Cytokinetics (CYTK) to develop aficamten in Greater China.
  • The oral cardiac myosin inhibitor is currently under priority review at China’s National Medical Products Administration as a treatment for obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, a disease of heart muscles.

data.title: VICI Properties closes $750M senior unsecured notes offering
data.description:
• VICI Properties (NYSE:VICI) has closed its public offering of $750M in aggregate principal amount of 5.125% senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • The notes were issued at 99.643% of par value and will mature on November 15, 2031.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tesla releases software update to address recall of 694K vehicles
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) issued a software update as part of a recall of 694,304 vehicles by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration. The update was needed to address an issue with the warning light on the tire pressure monitoring system. The impacted vehicles had previously installed a software release that was not compliant with the tire pressure monitoring system malfunction system.

The recall action covered certain Model 3 (2017-2025), Model Y (2020-2025), and Cybertruck (2024) vehicles. As of December 13, Tesla (TSLA) has identified 76 warranty claims and one field report related to this condition. The electric vehicle maker said it is not aware of any collisions, injuries or fatalities related to this condition.

Beginning on November 15, vehicles in production received the software remedy described above. No action is necessary from owners of vehicles equipped with software release 2024.38.200 or a later release in production.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were down 5.1% in premarket trading.

data.title: UK regulator has concerns about Synopsys, Ansys merger, but deal could be approved
data.description:
A potential deal between electronic design automation company Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) and Ansys (NASDAQ:ANSS) could raise prices and lead to reduced competition, the U.K.'s antitrust agency said in its phase 1 findings on Friday. However, the agency also said it could approve the deal if its concerns are alleviated.

Synopsys shares fell 2.3% in premarket trading on Friday, while Ansys was down fractionally.

"Having assessed the evidence, the [Competition and Markets Authority] believes the merger could reduce choice for customers – which tend to be major companies that operate globally and in the UK – that rely on these software products," the agency said in a statement. "This could lead to a loss of innovation, lower quality software and/or higher prices which may then be passed onto UK businesses and consumers."

The CMA's concerns related to global register transfer level power consumption analysis; and the global optics software and global photonics software market.

The companies now have the opportunity to address the concerns, or the deal will be referred to a phase 2 investigation.

Synopsys issued a statement and said it has already taken steps to address the concerns raised by the CMA in its phase 1 investigation, including the decision to sell its Optical Solutions business to Keysight Technologies (KEYS).

"We will continue our constructive and collaborative engagement with the CMA in relation to our proposed remedies," Synopsys added in the statement.

The CMA announced in October that the deal would be investigated for antitrust concerns. Earlier this month, the companies announced several remedies aimed at winning approval in the European Union.

Synopsys announced in January that it agreed to buy software developer Ansys for about $35B in cash and stock. Under the terms of the deal, Ansys shareholders will receive $197 in cash and 0.3450 shares of Synopsys common stock.

data.title: Hyzon expected to reduce workforce
data.description:
• Hydrogen fuel cell system manufacturer and technology developer Hyzon (NASDAQ:HYZN) is expected to effect a reduction in workforce.
  • HYZN shares were trading -27.71% Friday pre-market at $1.20.
  • The company cited its inability to raise funding and the future uncertainty relating to the availability of government subsidies, most notably the California Hybrid and Zero-Emission Truck and Bus Voucher Incentive Project, which is believed to have caused certain of its customers to slow down or suspend their purchasing decisions.
  • Hyzon has issued a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act, or WARN Act, notice to employees in Bolingbrook, Illinois and Troy, and Michigan.
  • The reduction in workforce is expected to be completed in February 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Knife River enters definitive agreement to acquire Strata
data.description:
• Knife River (NYSE:KNF) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Strata, a construction materials and contracting services provider in North Dakota and northwestern Minnesota.
  • Based on Strata’s projected 2025 Adj. EBITDA, the purchase price of $454M, reflects a multiple in the high single digits.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2025.
  • “We expect Strata to be accretive to Knife River’s Adj. EBITDA margin within the first year. Strata’s high-quality assets allow us to expand our service territory within a region we know well. We believe this is a great, long-term fit for us.” said Knife River President and CEO Brian Gray.

data.title: Humacyte surges on FDA approval for Symvess vascular repair product
data.description:
Humacyte (NASDAQ:HUMA) shares climbed ~57% in the premarket on Friday after the bioengineered tissue maker announced the FDA approval of Symvess, an off-the-shelf, implantable vascular conduit indicated for arterial injury.

According to the FDA labeling, Symvess will be available for adults with arterial injury in extremities. It will be indicated when an autologous vein graft is not feasible when there is a risk of near-term limb loss, and in other cases for urgent revascularization.

“SYMVESS approval in this first indication for arterial injury repair is a milestone for regenerative medicine overall, as well as for Humacyte,” CEO Laura Niklason remarked.

The Durham, North Carolina-based company said it has already recruited and trained sales personnel in preparation for the product’s commercial launch.

HUMA’s marketing application for Symvess is backed by real-world data and results from its V005 pivotal Phase 2/3 clinical study, which met its primary goals in patients with arterial injuries due to traumatic events such as gunshot wounds.

data.title: CCC Intelligent to acquire EvolutionIQ for $730M, effect up to $300M stock buybacks
data.description:
Software company, CCC Intelligent Solutions (NASDAQ:CCCS), is set to acquire EvolutionIQ for $730M.

EvolutionIQ is a platform for AI-powered guidance on disability and injury claims management.

The acquisition consideration is a combination of ~40% CCCS shares of common stock and 60% cash.

The cash consideration will be funded through cash on the balance sheet and an additional $225M term loan.

The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025.

Additionally, CCC Intelligent is set to effect an up to $300M share repurchase program.

The repurchase will be funded out of available liquidity and free cash flow.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Winnebago falls after warning on subdued demand; other leisure stocks also lower
data.description:
Winnebago (NYSE:WGO) fell in early trading on Friday after the company missed consensus estimates with its FQ1 earnings report.

The company said the RV and marine operating environment remained challenging in the first quarter, marked by subdued consumer demand and a cautious dealer network reluctant to make significant commitments on new orders ahead of the historically slow winter season.

CEO Michael Happer said the company ha a strategic focus on disciplined production, effective cost management and targeted investments in new products and technologies. "These strategies, complemented by our healthy balance sheet, prudent capital spending and robust liquidity, enhance our competitive position for an anticipated market recovery in the second half of fiscal 2025," he noted.

During FQ1, revenue fell 18.0% year-over-year to $625.6 million, driven primarily by lower unit volume and a reduction in average selling price per unit related to product mix. Winnebago's (WGO) gross profit margin decreased 290 basis points during the quarter to 12.3%, reflecting deleverage, higher warranty experience compared to the prior year and product mix, partially offset by operational efficiencies. EPS was reported at -$0.03 vs. $0.20 consensus and $0.95 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Winnebago (WGO) still sees full-year revenue of $2.9 billion to $3.2 billion (midpoint $3.05 billion) vs. $2.99 billion consensus and full-year EPS of $3.10 to $4.40 (midpoint $3.75) vs. a prior view for $3.00 to $4.50 and $3.38 consensus.

Shares of Winnebago (WGO) were down 4.39% in premarket trading to $49.65. THOR Industries (THO) was down 1.83% and LCI Industries (LCII) shed 1.05%. Brunswick (BC), Polaris (PII), and BRP (DOOO) are on watch as stocks that tend to swing in the same direction as Winnebago (WGO) historically.
